zh.Ailly-sur-Noye, Somme, Hauts-de-France,: 法國豪-德-福朗斯索姆艾利-素-諾也
zh.lifeSummary: 當Madeleine D’Ailly Sur Noye於1147年出生在法國豪-德-福朗斯索姆艾利-素-諾也時，她的父親Robert D’Ailly Sur Noye32歲，母親Clotilde De Breteuil17歲。她和Auguste De Gournay Sur Aronde至少有1個兒子。她去世於1218年享年71歲。

zh-hans.Ailly-sur-Noye, Somme, Hauts-de-France,: 法国豪-德-福朗斯索姆艾利-素-諾也
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Madeleine D’Ailly Sur Noye于1147年出生在法国豪-德-福朗斯索姆艾利-素-諾也时，她的父亲Robert D’Ailly Sur Noye32岁，母亲Clotilde De Breteuil17岁。她和Auguste De Gournay Sur Aronde至少有1个儿子。她去世于1218年享年71岁。

nameMeanings.name: D’Ailly
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and Irish: mainly Scottish variant of Irish   Daly  .
Scottish (of Norman origin): variant of   Dally   2, a cognate of 3 below.

variants.name: Daly
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Dermot, Brigid, Aileen, Declan, Finbarr, Fintan, Aidan, Brennan, Bridie, Cathal.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Dálaigh ‘descendant of Dálach’, a personal name based on dál (modern dáil) ‘meeting, assembly’.
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Dally   2.

variants.name: Alyea
meanings: Americanized form of French D'Ailly or Dailly . Compare Allee 2 and Elyea .

History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with several variants, including Allée, and the original form d'Ailly). The Huguenot ancestor was Nicolas d'Ailly, who came from France to NY c. 1682 via the Netherlands and England and died in NJ c. 1700.

nameMeanings.name: Sur
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Abha, Amit, Gopa, Partha, Prabhat, Sandip, Sanjiv, Shakti, Shyama, Sonali, Sumana, Sumit.  Korean Chae, Jin, Jong, Kheng, Ki Suk, Soon, Sun Kyu, Wan.
German: variant of   Sauer  .
Indian (West Bengal) and Bangladeshi: nickname from Sanskrit šūra ‘brave’.

variants.name: Sauer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Heinz, Otto, Achim, Dieter, Erwin, Hans, Helmut, Reinhold, Erna, Florian, Fritz.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for an embittered or cantankerous person, from Middle High German sūr, German sauer ‘sour’. In Jewish families, it is mainly artificial, assigned at random by Austrian clerks. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), the Netherlands, Denmark, Poland, Czechia, Croatia, and Slovenia.
Czech and Slovenian (Šauer): from German Schauer . In Slovenia, it is in part of Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) origin.

nameMeanings.name: Noye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre.
English (Cornwall): variant of   Noy  .
French (Noyé): variant of Noyer .

variants.name: Noy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Moshe, Eliezer, Ilan, Yacov, Yael, Yigal, Zahava.  Spanish Jose, Juan, Alfredo, Ana, Eugenio, Jesus, Luis, Mario, Miguel, Ruberto.
English: variant of   Noe  .

Jewish (Israeli): artificial name from Hebrew noy ‘decoration, adornment’, in part adopted as a Hebraicized form of various Ashkenazic surnames containing the unrelated German element neu, e.g. Neumann (see Newman ).

nameMeanings.name: Madeleine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The French form of the byname of a character in the New Testament, Mary Magdalene ‘Mary of Magdala’. Magdala was a village on Lake Galilee, a few miles north of Tiberias. The woman ‘which had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities’ (Luke 8:2) was given this name in the Bible to distinguish her from other bearers of the very common name Mary . It was widely accepted in Christian folk belief that she was the same person as the repentant sinner who washed Christ's feet with her tears in the previous chapter (Luke 7), but there is no support in the text for this identification.

variants.name: Madaline
meanings: The French form of the byname of a character in the New Testament, Mary Magdalene ‘Mary of Magdala’. Magdala was a village on Lake Galilee, a few miles north of Tiberias. variants.name: Madelaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Madeleine .

variants.name: Madoline
meanings: Modern respelling of   Madeleine .

variants.name: Madlyn
meanings: Modern variant of Madeleine , influenced by the productive suffix -lyn; see Lynn .

meanings: The French form of the byname of a character in the New Testament, Mary Magdalene ‘Mary of Magdala’. Magdala was a village on Lake Galilee, a few miles north of Tiberias. The woman ‘which had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities’ (Luke 8:2) was given this name in the Bible to distinguish her from other bearers of the very common name Mary . It was widely accepted in Christian folk belief that she was the same person as the repentant sinner who washed Christ's feet with her tears in the previous chapter (Luke 7), but there is no support in the text for this identification.

en.lifeSummary: When Madeleine D’Ailly Sur Noye was born in 1147, in Ailly-sur-Noye, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France, her father, Robert D’Ailly Sur Noye, was 32 and her mother, Clotilde De Breteuil, was 17. She had at least 1 son with Auguste De Gournay Sur Aronde. She died in 1218, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Madeleine D’Ailly Sur Noye est née en 1147, à Ailly-sur-Noye, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France, son père, Robert D’Ailly Sur Noye, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Clotilde De Breteuil, avait 17 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Auguste De Gournay Sur Aronde. Elle est décédée en 1218, à l’âge de 71 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Madeleine D’Ailly Sur Noye 1147 in Ailly-sur-Noye, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert D’Ailly Sur Noye 32 und ihre Mutter Clotilde De Breteuil 17. Sie hatte mit Auguste De Gournay Sur Aronde mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1218 im Alter von 71 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Madeleine D’Ailly Sur Noye è nata il nel 1147 nel Ailly-sur-Noye, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia, sua padre, Robert D’Ailly Sur Noye, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Clotilde De Breteuil, aveva 17 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Auguste De Gournay Sur Aronde. Morì nel 1218, all'età di 71 anni.

ja.Ailly-sur-Noye, Somme, Hauts-de-France,: フランスハウトス-デ-フランスソンメエイリ-スル-ノイ
ja.lifeSummary: Madeleine D’Ailly Sur Noyeが1147年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスソンメエイリ-スル-ノイで生まれた時，父Robert D’Ailly Sur Noyeは32歳，母Clotilde De Breteuilは17歳であった。彼女はAuguste De Gournay Sur Arondeとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1218年に71歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Madeleine D’Ailly Sur Noye(이)가 1147년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 솜 아이리-수-노예에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert D’Ailly Sur Noye(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Clotilde De Breteuil(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 Auguste De Gournay Sur Aronde(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1218년 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Madeleine D’Ailly Sur Noye nasceu em 1147, em Ailly-sur-Noye, Somme, Hauts-de-France, França, seu pai, Robert D’Ailly Sur Noye, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Clotilde De Breteuil, tinha 17 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Auguste De Gournay Sur Aronde. Ela faleceu em 1218, com 71 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Madeleine D’Ailly Sur Noye родилась в 1147 г. в местности эилли-сур-нои, сомм, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция, её отцу по имени Robert D’Ailly Sur Noye было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Clotilde De Breteuil, 17. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Auguste De Gournay Sur Aronde. Она умерла в 1218 г. в возрасте 71 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Madeleine D’Ailly Sur Noye nació en 1147, en Ailly-sur-Noye, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia, su papá, Robert D’Ailly Sur Noye, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Clotilde De Breteuil, tenía 17 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Auguste De Gournay Sur Aronde. Murió en 1218, a la edad de 71 años.
